9E1AA2F0EEEE2653D710B9DBD774BEAF: Counters can be used in conjunction with automated actions in 2 ways: as a trigger condition, and as an action response. As a trigger condition, counters can be used to trigger actions when their value becomes less than, greater than, or equal to a certain value. As an action response, the automated action can increment or decrement the counter's value by a specific number, or set the counter's value to a specific number.

043B3E1FFB15AD55C24F64104E776DFA: The "Encrypted Security PIN" and "Encrypted Password" fields will only accept encrypted data strings that have been exported via the Export User CSV dialog or from the Inception REST API. This option is provided to allow cloning User data from one Inception system to another without having to restore an entire database backup, as plain-text PINs and passwords cannot be exported from Inception for security reasons. To input plain-text PINs or passwords in the CSV import, use the "Security PIN" or "Password" fields instead. Avoid using the encrypted and unencrypted variants of the same input field when entering user data.

1D69F06031AE69D14851D0202F7D0BD3: Permanently isolated inputs will not report alarms or tampers and can only be de-isolated by a user. Often used for inputs that are faulty or having maintenance done to them, these inputs should be repaired and de-isolated when possible.
1E5A46588632F634F083B590F8E6FA71: Allows users to be notified when a user successfully logs into the Inception web interface. The notification will include the name of the user who logged on.
2044E29D045F6B7476DB44EE6C7C4E3E: will show the currently linked account. The chain icon on the right of the field allows a user to be linked to an IR Connect account.
2048A156F7ADCE888E9C31AC4B462FA6: Alternatively, you can use a combination of these two methods by assigning the user one or more permission groups based on their role and then allowing or denying additional items on a per user basis. The benefit of this is that it reduces the overall number of permission groups required, with the core permissions that a number of users share forming a permission group and any exceptions to that rule being applied to the individual users. Permission groups can also contain additional permission groups, which may be used when a role in the company requires the same permissions as another, as well as additional permissions. For example, warehouse managers could have a permission group created including the warehouse staff permission group, as well as the other permissions required as a manager. Multiple permission groups could also be combined together in this way.

2887D7BD20131EF23D409939ACFBB7E4: will determine what is displayed for the user on the homepage of the Inception web interface. The dashboard content provides options to show shortcuts to common pages grouped by the type of action. For a shortcut to appear on the dashboard, the web profile also needs to include the correct permission to the page the shortcut relates to.
2BFEE43D675860F1D7DD5D50A487CCF9: It is recommended that when creating or modifying profiles, the profile should only be able to view and interact with the pages that are actually required, with the rest being hidden away. This results in an uncluttered and easy to use interface with unimportant items for that user hidden away.
2D542EF7A89267FE2BDB03DAE8AD1520: Inception supports sending messages to users via email. To use the email notification path, Inception must be configured with an email server to use and the recipient users must have an email address assigned to them.
2DBA13DFE8AC49A94EE87A860F161E15: Allows the selection of the the type of Wiegand keypad. The type selected will determine how information about PIN key presses are interpreted.
3186292C60AD97EDA31C20677BE8A6F8: - Generates a report which shows the list of item permissions and Permission Groups that each User has been assigned. Has the option of generating different levels of detail for reports, depending on how much information is required. Optionally filterable by Users and Items.
32A16EA77172790E36B7349BD3DCE84A: This page is used to configure the users that interact with the Inception Controller and the Inception web interface. This includes their credentials and user permissions.
364DB6E1A8D350B875AB38D94F013A8A: Entries for the "Permissions" field are only allowed to be the name of an existing Permission Group in the system (case-insensitive); user permissions for individual items cannot be assigned through CSV import.
383A4BF7665C75791BB0303F73141EDA: If enabled, this user will be restricted from accessing the system in any way, similar to user expiry above. This option is automatically enabled when the above When to Cancel User option is used.
3B766ACE1065422076D9B08C968BAF5E: When an anti-passback violation occurs a review event will be automatically logged, a system message (a blue message on the System Overview page requiring acknowledgement) will be created and optionally notifications to users can be sent.

3FB6E27EF8FD490A5CED998D057E2084: " for the specific field. This is different to not specifying a value for a field, which will leave the field unchanged for the user. 4094240216B869479B780A6608DF168A: The IR Connect account that is linked to this user. Click the link button to enter the user's IR Connect account (email address). If they don't have an IR Connect account, an email will be automatically sent instructing them how to set up their free account.
4381A716FA8D16D6E1661BBD483D1BE3: Setting this value to 0 will disable lockouts, meaning that any individual user may attempt as many times to login, but the default lockout will still be present for any unknown or erroneous usernames.
4462D86DBBE7CADD1483A504B9F7D339: Successfully imported files are deleted from the network share folder after the user data is imported. Files that fail to import are moved into a user-specified subfolder, or deleted if no subfolder is given. If the DUIM service fails to import a file, a system message will be created with the details of the error to help identify why the import failed, so the file can be amended and reimported if required.
44B5ADB1BE4AC7398C2A209F696AB603: Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) is not enabled. 2FA can be enabled on the [Configuration > General > System] page. The Manage Users page must be refreshed once 2FA is enabled.
44B7437DD9AE9B90784327EACB9700E7: If enable, mobile credentials of this user will be revoked upon passing User Expiry time.By default, this option will be unchecked.
464A1998ABB8CA4AFC4CFD475872A6C8: If enabled, the user can update the firmware of the Inception Controller, LAN Modules or peripherals from the
467F2C2DEF838FD1A7C66C3964B7A87C: Some System Warnings and Messages can only be cleared by a user with special permissions, which means that LCD Terminals may keep beeping periodically and flashing LEDs until the warnings/messages are cleared. If the fault cannot be fixed right away, the "Mute" button on this dialog can stop the periodic beeping until the warning/message can be attended to. (Note: New Messages/Warnings will cause the LCD Terminal to start to draw attention again.)
4833CC0109057F8A93867C2D1EF234F6: Import user photos in bulk by uploading a ZIP file containing multiple JPEG image files inside. Image filenames are matched up with users based on the user's Name, so it is recommended that user names are unique.
486926709FAA87212A108922854D09E0: When a user property is selected, a custom column name can be entered which will be treated as the original user property. During the import process, user property data under that alternate column name will be imported as if it was for the original user property.
49889D3F20F048F420B22A1C33325DDE: Caution: Permission to execute this task should only be provided to the installer. Updating of firmware may include additional tasks that need to be followed which, if not performed correctly, can compromise site security.
4AA6B51747D2A840D21C336B1C1C8FC8: Specifies the user's password to use for network/domain authentication.
4C2CC2BDF5487742091354EA3E4E5092: The Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) enrolment status of the user. 2FA-enrolled users must enter an extra security code from a personal device before they can log into the web interface. 56AD5CC14EB526233F5DA15E99627BED: Enabling any of the emergency buttons will cause the Emergency button to be shown on the Touchscreen's main screen, which will open a menu with the enabled options when tapped. It will also automatically create a calculated input for this touchscreen associated with that emergency button. The calculated input will be pulsed when the button on the touchscreen's Emergency menu is activated by a user.

5C6E98A6D2638D8C510DC745F4C64F67: Scheduled tasks are items that automatically execute system tasks on a specific schedule, and then send the results to the user(s) who require them. For example, you could use a scheduled task to schedule a database backup every 2 weeks on a Sunday night at 11:00pm, and have the backup file emailed to the system administrator.
5CDC530D9C93BD34AE17D52A999B39EE: This option is used for users who require extra time to select a floor, like a delivery driver bringing in parcels or a user with a disability or impaired mobility.
5E0A77B374AA3B32CFEE48229A6928AB: When a user property is selected, a custom column name can be entered which will be treated as the original user property when imported. 5F50EFC39619491592C1ADFE2B71C3ED: This page is used to configure web page profiles. Web page profiles determine the level of functionality a user has on the Inception web interface.

7722A14478129C835BFE9E089ECCF2AC: option is enabled, this setting will determine how long Inception will attempt to resend failed push notifications generated by this Notifier (e.g. due to network failure). You can configure a longer retry time (up to 24 hours) for more important notifications to make sure they are sent through after network issues get resolved. If any notifications cannot be successfully sent within the retry time limit, they will expire and an error will be logged to the events page. Note that this retry time limit only applies to valid push notifications that fail due to network or SkyTunnel service issues; notifications that fail due to missing IR Connect user IDs or missing subscriptions will be discarded.

7E8B6E701A401CBBED6182F4404844B3: The security PIN can also be used to log onto the web interface instead of using a password. The security PIN will not be visible once the user is saved, if the user has forgotten their security PIN, you may press the “Send PIN” button. This will open a new window which allows an email with the Security PIN and basic details to be sent to the user. Otherwise, another user with higher privileges (such as an installer) with “Reveal PIN” permissions may use this button to reveal and view the PIN.
7EF8466A4453C66A8627D9A360D55C75: Once a user has initially enrolled for 2FA, the enrolment dialog will no longer be accessible by clicking the link button for security reasons, the 2FA configuration will have to be deleted and re-created in order to re-enrol.

8F8E18EBAAC8047DED3F9DC86E175A5B: System messages are informational, telling the user about events that happened in the past such as a system reset or a health issue that has been restored, and just need to be acknowledged to remove. 8FE6B87356F2B8908DA6070B84037FD2: Enter the user's email address to link this user with an IR Connect account. If the user does not have an IR Connect account already, they will be automatically emailed an invitation to the address entered. The invitation email includes an account activation link and information to assist the user on how to install the IR Connect App on their smartphone.
902CA349A55B4F80B748A37EBE7ABB4A: Remote fob templates are used when assigning remote fobs to users. The remote fob template contains the type of remote and describes what each button on the remote will do. When assigning a remote fob to a user, the remote fob template is selected and the serial number of the remote fob is typed in. This allows multiple users to be given remotes that have the same button behaviour and if any behaviour changes are required it can be edited in one place. Multiple remote fob templates can also be created if different users require different button behaviours.
902D9FFDBA5D741800E1FF7A426793BA: Not all details can be synchronised with full compatibility to the ILAM offline database (such as non-Time Period permissions), so a warning is generated whenever something synchronised does not fully match the Inception system.

919BC596CC03D86FCA7827D7A3D4900B: Import users in bulk by uploading a CSV file. Users can either be added or updated by matching on the user Name in the CSV. As updating of users is keyed on Name, it is recommended that user names are unique.
92CF83EBE84AE6D8FB021DDDF1043A63: This page is used to configure terminal profiles. Terminal profiles determine the level of functionality a user has on the LCD Terminal.

9E48D5659AADB37BF673533E570A1EA9: This dialog allows the generation and copying of an API Token which will be associated with this user. 9EE781AB385DD8E67B6598E115FBBC54: The CSV file must contain a header line that specifies the User data columns that are to be imported, and the column titles must match the User properties as they appear on the Manage User page. 9FEB02CF09692713AE95635B8DC98EE9: The recommended format for date/time field data (e.g. User Expiry Time) is "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS". This avoids any ambiguity with the date format. C2C5C27C0EED7F91AE209BC84F96CBC0: Opens a menu that allows for the import of user photos in bulk through uploading a ZIP file with JPEG image files. The image files should be named to the corresponding user the photo will be assigned to.

The uploaded zip file cannot exceed 100MB, larger batches of images may have to be split into multiple zip files.

D067BAE3597D2F3E2FE1CB5C4EAE15F2: Battery tests can abort for a number of reasons, a user can cancel the test, AC was lost on the power supply, a wire was shorted, comms to the module was lost or the module needed to sound sirens.
